title: New skill: find out how accurate Redfin's estimate actually is
body: Redfin told a potential buyer that a San Francisco home was worth $600K more than its asking price. Should they trust that? We built a new skill that answers that question. You paste a for-sale listing URL and it checks how accurate Redfin's estimates have been on nearby homes that already sold — using actual sale prices, not guesses. If Redfin has been overshooting by 12% in that area, you'll know before you overbid. This is a different tool from the original Redfin Estimate Checker, which compares Redfin's prediction to the final sale price on homes that have already closed. That one tells you how Redfin did after the fact. This one tells you how much to trust Redfin right now, on a home you're actually considering.

title: Skill pages now tell you exactly what to do
body: The old skill pages told you what each skill does but not how to actually get it working. That gap is fixed. Each skill page now has numbered step-by-step instructions — from downloading the Claude app all the way through the exact phrase to type when you're ready to use it. The skill files also now download with a proper name (like redfin_estimate_checker.md) instead of a generic filename that's impossible to find in your Downloads folder.

title: Skill cards are easier to use
body: Three small improvements to the skill cards on the homepage. The entire card is now clickable — you don't have to aim for the small button. The button itself now says 'Get the skill' which is clearer about what happens next. And we removed the 'Need help installing?' link from the homepage — it belongs on the individual skill page after you've already decided you want it, not before.

title: You can now contact us without leaving the site
body: Before today, every "Get in touch" button on this site opened your email app and dropped you into a blank compose window. That's a lousy experience — most people just close it and move on. We replaced all of those with a real contact form at /contact. You fill it out, hit send, and we get a notification. No email client required. First lead that comes through it will be a good day.

title: Site launched with two real estate skills
body: Went live with the first version of thebotcandoit.com and two free skills built specifically for real estate professionals. The first one downloads all the photos from any listing page — Redfin, Zillow, Compass, Realtor.com — to a folder on your computer in about 30 seconds. The second reverse-engineers Redfin's valuation model to show exactly which features of a property are dragging its estimate down and by how much. Both were built because these are tasks that realtors, buyers, and investors actually do manually, repeatedly, every week. The site itself was built by one person using Claude — which is the point.
